Description:
Integrate OpenTelemetry distributed tracing, Grafana dashboards, Prometheus + Loki, Sentry error tracking, and an observability agent for AI-specific metrics.

SCOPE: MVP 3
PROJECT AREA: Observability
STORIES: 13.1 - 13.5

Integrate OpenTelemetry distributed tracing and metrics across all agents and FastAPI routers.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Install opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp
- Configure TracerProvider with OTLP exporter (endpoint from OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var)
- Auto-instrument FastAPI with FastAPIInstrumentor
- Add manual spans in each agent.run() method (span name: {agent_id}.run)
- Trace attributes: agent_id, job_count, duration_ms, token_usage
- Configure MeterProvider for metrics: jobs_scraped_total, ranking_duration_seconds, llm_tokens_used_total
- Propagate trace context through Temporal activities

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Observability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-13, JD-50

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Traces visible in OTLP-compatible backend (Jaeger for local dev)
- FastAPI request spans created automatically
- Agent spans nested under request span
- Metrics emitted and scrapeable by Prometheus

EDGE CASES:
- OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT missing: fall back to console exporter in dev mode
- Trace context must cross Temporal activity boundary

Create Grafana dashboards for latency p50/p95, token usage, hallucination rate, retrieval precision, and reranker confidence.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Dashboard 1: API Latency - p50 and p95 per endpoint, error rate
- Dashboard 2: AI Agent Performance - token usage per agent, LLM call duration, retry rate
- Dashboard 3: RAG Quality - retrieval precision, context recall, hallucination alert count
- Dashboard 4: Ranking - reranker confidence distribution, similarity score histogram
- Dashboard 5: Infrastructure - container CPU/memory, active connections, DLQ depth
- Export dashboards as JSON for version control in infrastructure/grafana/
- Configure Prometheus as data source

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Observability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-62

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 5 dashboards render without errors
- Metrics populated from Prometheus
- Hallucination alert fires when rate > 1%

EDGE CASES:
- Dashboard JSONs must be importable into any Grafana 10+ instance
- Avoid dashboard IDs that conflict with default Grafana dashboards

Configure Prometheus metrics scraping and Loki log aggregation from structured JSON stdout.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Prometheus: scrape FastAPI /metrics endpoint (via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator)
- Configure scrape_interval=15s for agent metrics
- Loki: configure Promtail to read container stdout (Docker log driver)
- Loki labels: container_name, source_id, log_level
- Structured JSON logs parsed by Loki pipeline stages (json stage extracting level, agent, message)
- Add loki_query examples to docs/OBSERVABILITY.md

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Observability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-62, JD-15

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Prometheus scrapes /metrics successfully
- Loki receives and indexes container logs
- Log queries by agent name work in Loki
- Metrics appear in Grafana dashboards

EDGE CASES:
- Promtail must handle multi-line log entries (JSON spanning multiple lines not expected but guard anyway)
- Prometheus cardinality: avoid high-cardinality labels (e.g. job_id per metric)

Integrate Sentry for error tracking and Microsoft Clarity for frontend session replay.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Backend: install sentry-sdk; configure in main.py with SENTRY_DSN from settings
- Capture all unhandled exceptions automatically via Sentry FastAPI integration
- Add Sentry trace_sample_rate=0.1 (10% of requests traced)
- Frontend: install @sentry/nextjs; wrap app with Sentry.init in instrumentation.ts
- Microsoft Clarity: add Clarity project ID script to app/layout.tsx head
- NEXT_PUBLIC_CLARITY_PROJECT_ID from env var
- Do NOT capture PII in Sentry (scrub email, CV content from error context)

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Observability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-13, JD-24

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Unhandled exception appears in Sentry dashboard within 30 seconds
- Frontend errors captured by Sentry
- Clarity session replay active on dashboard page
- PII scrubbing verified (no email/CV data in Sentry events)

EDGE CASES:
- Sentry SDK must be initialised before any other middleware
- Clarity script must load after consent if GDPR applies

Build backend/agents/observability/observability_agent.py for AI-specific reliability monitoring.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Monitor schema conformance rate: alert if < 99% of LLM outputs match expected schema
- Monitor hallucination rate: alert if > 1% of RAG answers flagged by DeepEval faithfulness check
- Monitor retrieval precision: alert if < 0.80 (real-time metric, not CI-only)
- Monitor token budget: alert if any agent exceeds CONTRACT.md token budget by > 20%
- Expose /api/v1/observability/status endpoint returning all metrics as JSON
- Run as background task (asyncio periodic task every 5 minutes)

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Observability / AI Quality
DEPENDENCIES: JD-62, JD-45

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Schema conformance alert fires on test violation
- Hallucination alert fires on test RAG output
- /api/v1/observability/status returns all metrics
- Periodic task runs every 5 minutes without blocking HTTP worker

EDGE CASES:
- Observability agent itself must not cause outage - wrap in try/except, log errors but continue
- Metrics must be computed incrementally not by scanning entire database

Labels:
Implement Supabase Auth with JWT validation, Row-Level Security policies, OWASP Top 10 hardening, and GDPR compliance features.

SCOPE: MVP 3
PROJECT AREA: Security / Auth
STORIES: 14.1 - 14.4

Description:
Implement JWT validation middleware in FastAPI with RBAC enforced via JWT claims.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Install python-jose for JWT validation
- Create backend/middleware/auth.py: extract Bearer token from Authorization header; validate signature using Supabase JWT secret; decode claims
- Claims: sub (user ID), role (user/admin), email
- Add HTTPBearer dependency to all protected routes
- RBAC: admin claim required for /api/v1/admin/* endpoints; user claim for all others
- Unauthenticated requests return 401; insufficient role returns 403

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Security
DEPENDENCIES: JD-14, JD-49

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Valid JWT allows access to protected routes
- Expired JWT returns 401
- Admin route with user-role JWT returns 403
- Invalid signature returns 401

EDGE CASES:
- JWT clock skew: allow 30 second leeway in expiry validation
- Service-to-service calls (Temporal activities): use service account token

Configure Supabase RLS policies so users can only read and write their own rows.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Enable RLS on all tables: jobs, applications, cv, cover_letter, interview_prep
- Policy: USING (auth.uid() = user_id) for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
- Jobs table: readable by all authenticated users (jobs are shared); writeable only by service role
- Applications, CV, CoverLetter: user-scoped read/write policies
- Admin bypass: service role key bypasses RLS for admin operations
- Test policies via Supabase SQL Editor before deployment

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Database / Security
DEPENDENCIES: JD-40, JD-67

TESTING CRITERIA:
- User A cannot read User B's applications
- Job listings readable by all authenticated users
- Service role can insert jobs (scraper agent)
- RLS policy SQL tested and documented

EDGE CASES:
- RLS must be enabled before data is populated in production
- Supabase Realtime must respect RLS (verify per channel subscription)

Run Trivy and Bandit security scans in CI, configure SSRF allowlist, and integrate Azure Key Vault for secret management.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add trivy-scan job to ci.yml: trivy image job-discovery:ci --exit-code 1 --severity HIGH,CRITICAL
- Add bandit-scan job: uv run bandit -r backend/ -ll (fail on medium+ issues)
- SSRF allowlist: define ALLOWED_EXTERNAL_DOMAINS in settings.py; validate all outbound HTTP URLs in security agent
- Azure Key Vault: reference secrets as Key Vault URI in Container App config (no plaintext secrets in env or Terraform state)
- Add security scanning results as CI artifacts

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Security / Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-53, JD-49

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Trivy scan runs on every docker build
- Bandit scan fails on high-severity issues
- SSRF test: request to non-allowlisted URL rejected with 400
- Key Vault references resolve correctly in production

EDGE CASES:
- Trivy false positives: add .trivyignore for accepted findings with justification
- Bandit: configure .bandit file to exclude known false positives

Implement data export, deletion, and audit logging for GDPR compliance.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Export endpoint: GET /api/v1/user/export - returns PDF/JSON/CSV of all user data (applications, CV, cover letters, interview prep)
- Deletion endpoint: DELETE /api/v1/user - deletes all rows from PostgreSQL, pgvector embeddings, Redis cache entries, and object storage uploads
- Audit log: write immutable audit event on every export and deletion to audit_log table (user_id, action, timestamp, ip_address)
- Retention: raw scrape data retained 90 days then purged by scheduled Temporal workflow
- Document data flows in docs/SECURITY.md

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Compliance
DEPENDENCIES: JD-40, JD-67

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Export returns all user-owned data in requested format
- Deletion removes all data including pgvector embeddings and Redis entries
- Audit log entry created for every export and deletion
- Deletion confirmed complete within 72 hours (GDPR requirement)

EDGE CASES:
- Partial deletion failure: log failed components, alert via Sentry, retry
- Export of large CV or cover letter data: stream response to avoid memory pressure

Complete Twelve-Factor App compliance audit, implement graceful shutdown, wire all admin tooling, and validate disaster recovery.

SCOPE: MVP 3
PROJECT AREA: Platform / Reliability
STORIES: 15.1 - 15.4

Audit all 12 factors against production config, document gaps, and close them.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Review each factor against production Azure Container Apps deployment
- Factor I: verify single codebase, no per-environment branches
- Factor III: verify no secrets in codebase (run git-secrets scan)
- Factor VI: verify no in-process session state (fake_db must be removed)
- Factor VIII: verify Temporal worker scales independently of uvicorn
- Factor XI: verify all agents use get_logger (no print() calls - run grep check in CI)
- Document audit results in docs/ENGINEERING-STANDARDS.md
- Close any identified gaps before MVP 3 release

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Platform
DEPENDENCIES: All previous epics

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 12 factors documented in ENGINEERING-STANDARDS.md with compliance status
- No gaps remain open at MVP 3 release
- git-secrets scan passes (no committed secrets)
- grep for print( in backend/ returns zero results

EDGE CASES:
- Factor VI gap (fake_db): must be resolved by Story 8.1 completion
- Factor XI gap: CI step added to fail on print() usage

Implement SIGTERM handlers in all long-running processes: uvicorn drain, Temporal checkpoint, Playwright session close.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- uvicorn: configure graceful_timeout=30 in supervisord stopwaitsecs
- Temporal workers: handle SIGTERM by stopping acceptance of new activities; drain in-progress activities; checkpoint state
- Playwright agents: register SIGTERM handler (signal.signal) calling asyncio.ensure_future(browser.close())
- Supervisor: stopwaitsecs=30 for all programs
- Test graceful shutdown: docker stop (sends SIGTERM); verify clean shutdown in logs

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Platform / Reliability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-50, JD-21, JD-22

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docker stop results in clean shutdown (exit 0) for all processes
- In-flight HTTP requests complete before uvicorn exits
- Playwright browser closed cleanly on SIGTERM (no zombie chromium processes)
- Temporal activity state checkpointed

EDGE CASES:
- Browser.close() may hang if page is frozen - add asyncio.wait_for timeout of 10s
- SIGKILL sent by Docker after 30s - all cleanup must complete within 25s

Fully wire replay_dlq.py and run_evals.py admin scripts; add schedule pause/resume routes.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- replay_dlq.py: implement full retry logic using Temporal client to requeue workflow; accept --id or --all flag; log result per item
- run_evals.py: integrate DeepEval + Ragas; accept --agent and --format (json/text) flags; write results to evals/{agent}/results-{timestamp}.json
- GET /api/v1/admin/schedule: return list of active Temporal cron workflows with next run time
- POST /api/v1/admin/schedule/{workflow_id}/pause: pause Temporal cron workflow
- POST /api/v1/admin/schedule/{workflow_id}/resume: resume paused workflow
- All admin routes require admin JWT claim

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Admin
DEPENDENCIES: JD-52, JD-38, JD-50

TESTING CRITERIA:
- replay_dlq.py --all requeues all DLQ items
- run_evals.py --format json produces valid JSON report
- Schedule pause/resume toggles Temporal cron state
- All scripts documented in docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md

EDGE CASES:
- replay_dlq.py with empty DLQ: log no items to replay and exit 0
- Pausing non-existent workflow returns 404

Execute backup restore drill for PostgreSQL WAL and Redis AOF; validate RTO <= 1 hour, RPO <= 15 minutes.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Document backup strategy in docs/RELIABILITY.md: PostgreSQL WAL via Supabase PITR, Redis AOF persistence
- Execute restore drill: disable production writes, restore from backup to staging environment, verify data integrity
- Measure RTO: time from failure detection to service restoration
- Measure RPO: maximum data age at restore point (target: <= 15 min)
- Document restore commands in docs/RELIABILITY.md with exact procedure
- Schedule quarterly DR drills - add to infrastructure/AGENT.md

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Platform / Reliability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-40, JD-71

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Restore drill completes within 1 hour (RTO met)
- Restored data not older than 15 minutes (RPO met)
- Restore procedure documented step-by-step
- Data integrity verified post-restore (row counts match, pgvector indexes intact)

EDGE CASES:
- Redis AOF may be large - test restore time with realistic data volume
- Supabase PITR requires paid plan - confirm plan includes PITR before relying on it
